planPrerequisites.description: pact-write must consume the persisted plan snapshot from disk before deciding what provider verification scaffolding is safe to write.

scanSupportInputs.description: pact-write must read the persisted scan snapshot from disk as supporting evidence for target files, provider state hints, and artifact-source honesty.

requiredMachineState.description: Canonical persisted write result for Pact provider verification scaffolding. It must capture the selected provider, the input plan verdict, actual writes performed, intentional skips, remaining blockers, manual follow-ups, and the next verification step without pretending readiness unsupported by persisted state.

planVerdictTaxonomy.description: pact-write must obey this persisted plan verdict taxonomy instead of inventing a new decision surface.

writeOutcomeTaxonomy.description: pact-write must distinguish completed scaffold writes, partial scaffold/remediation, blocked writes, and honest no-op outcomes.

repoWritePolicy: extend existing provider verification files when safe
create new provider-state-oriented scaffold files only when the persisted plan justifies them
allow minimal dependency/config patches only when clearly needed by the chosen scaffold
forbid broad refactors or unrelated file rewrites
ownership.description: pact-write may always update the canonical write state files above and may touch repo files only when the persisted plan and repo evidence make the target narrow, attributable, and safe.

stopConditions.description: Required persisted plan state is missing, so write cannot proceed honestly.
Required persisted scan state is missing, so write cannot reconcile target files and evidence honestly.
The persisted plan verdict is blocked, so write must not imply progress by generating scaffolding.
The persisted plan verdict is irrelevant, so Pact provider verification artifacts must not be written.
The safe provider verification target file or extension point cannot be identified without inventing a new architecture.

summaryState.description: Human-readable Pact write summary only. Canonical machine state stays in write-state.json.
